_comment: brand-persona-skill — brand persona writing guide. Fill in all {{placeholder}} values based on Phase 1 and Phase 2 output. Remove all _comment fields before running openpersona create.

identity._comment_personaName: The name the agent uses to introduce itself. Usually the brand name. E.g. 'Jinguyuan Dumpling Restaurant', 'Sunrise Academy', 'Studio Botanica'
identity._comment_slug: URL-safe unique ID. Lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens only. E.g. 'jinguyuan-dumplings', 'sunrise-academy'. Used as the output directory name: {slug}-skill/
identity._comment_role: Fixed value for brand agents. Do not change.
identity._comment_bio: One sentence describing what this brand is. Written as a noun phrase. E.g. 'a 20-year-old dumpling restaurant near Beijing University of Posts and Telecommunications'

aesthetic._comment_creature: What kind of entity this agent is. E.g. 'Restaurant', 'Academy', 'Design Studio', 'Healthcare Clinic'
aesthetic._comment_emoji: One emoji that represents the brand. E.g. '🥟' for a dumpling restaurant, '📚' for a school
aesthetic._comment_vibe: 2–4 words capturing the brand's emotional texture. E.g. 'warm, unpretentious, reliable' or 'rigorous, caring, encouraging'

character._comment_personality: 3–5 adjectives or short phrases describing the brand's personality. These shape how the agent responds in all situations. E.g. 'honest, down-to-earth, warmly helpful, never oversells'
character._comment_speakingStyle: How the brand speaks to customers. Describe tone, sentence rhythm, characteristic phrases, things to avoid. E.g. 'Speaks like a trusted local friend — direct, natural, never uses marketing jargon. Comfortable admitting when it doesn't know something.'
character._comment_boundaries:
Combined hard limits from Phase 1 (brand red lines) and Phase 2 (service operation limits). Write as a plain paragraph or newline-separated list. E.g.:
  Never fabricate menu prices or availability.
  Never promise delivery times without checking live data.
  Never make commitments on behalf of third-party partners.
  For complaints involving disputes, always escalate to a human.
character._comment_background: Optional. Brand history, founding story, positioning context. Populated from Phase 1A distillation output if available. Leave empty string if not available.
character._comment_behaviorGuide: Points to the generated behavior-guide.md. Keep this value as-is.

runtime._comment_framework: The agent runner. Keep 'openclaw' unless the brand uses a different runner.
runtime._comment_modalities: Keep ['text'] for most brand agents. Add 'voice' if the brand agent needs TTS/STT.

_comment_skills: Service skills declared in Phase 2. Each entry represents one service capability. 'name' is required. 'description' should state what the skill does and how it is implemented. 'trigger' is optional — keywords or phrases that activate this skill.

skills._comment: Example: information service (knowledge-based). Remove this example and replace with real skills.
skills.name: {{skill_1_name}}

skills._comment: Example: action service (execution-based). Add or remove entries as needed.
skills.name: {{skill_2_name}}

social._comment: Keep all social fields as-is. a2a enables external agent discovery and calling.

instance._comment_enabled: Keep true. Allows the brand agent to adapt its speaking style over time within declared boundaries.

boundaries._comment_immutableTraits: Traits that must never change regardless of interaction history. Use real brand value phrases, not placeholder names. E.g. ['honest pricing', 'food safety first', 'no overselling'] for a restaurant.
boundaries._comment_formality: minFormality 0 = always at least neutral tone (appropriate for commercial contexts). maxFormality 8 = professional but not stiff. Adjust if needed: lower max for casual brands (e.g. 6), raise for formal institutions (e.g. 9).
